campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies pay to operate in the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to the tax rates, and the council discussed and voted on whether to approve the new ordinance.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases for 2026
Finance department recommendations on tax adjustments
Council vote on new municipal code amendments
Impact on local businesses operating in Campbell

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents had a chance to speak about a new rule the city is considering. After hearing public input, the council introduced a new ordinance (city rule) and skipped the usual first reading process to move it along faster. The council then directed staff to put it on the consent calendar so it can be officially adopted at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on new ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to expedite the process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular council meeting
Council directed staff to place measure on consent calendar

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council authorized the Town Manager to sign off on several construction contracts and professional service agreements to move forward with town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other vendors
Approved town improvement projects and directed planning
Authorized professional service agreements (3-year terms)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met to approve meeting minutes and discussed several projects including replacing an old elevator at the terminal, finding companies to clean up homeless encampments, and updating an advertising agreement with Clear Channel for digital billboards on city property. The council also approved city support for a firefighter memorial event in District 7.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project - addressing infrastructure needs
Encampment Cleanup Services - request for proposals to address homelessness
Digital Signage Agreement - updating contract with advertising company for city billboards
Light the Night for Fallen Firefighters - memorial event in Council District 7

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes, hear directly from residents with public comments, and review neighborhood grant requests for the upcoming 2026-27 budget year. The council discussed which community projects should receive funding and heard updates on neighborhood meeting attendance and other staff reports.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27 fiscal year
Public comment period - residents spoke directly to the council
Neighborhood meeting attendance and community engagement
Approval of previous meeting minutes
Committee reports and staff updates

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to provide public comments either in person at City Hall or online during the session.
keyTopics: Board and Commission interviews - selecting new members for city advisory bodies
Public comment period - residents could speak in person or online
Study session format - focused discussion meeting rather than regular voting session

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a closed-door meeting to negotiate with the Cupertino Union School District about buying or selling property at 10480 Finch Avenue. The council also discussed the Silicon Valley Hopper program and plans to apply for future grant funding.
keyTopics: Negotiating purchase/sale of Finch Avenue property with the school district
Price and payment terms for the real estate deal
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be temporarily installed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also approved a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters that will run through 2029.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events
New 3-year contract agreed with Santa Clara Firefighters union (Local 1171)
One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to plan the city's spending for the next several years. They discussed the budget for 2026-27, approved a plan for new parks and trails in the Innovation District area, and made updates to how the council conducts its meetings.
keyTopics: 5-year capital spending plan for 2027-2031
2026-27 operating budget presentation
New parks and trails plan for Innovation District
Updates to City Council meeting rules and procedures

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to consider a proposal to divide a vacant lot on Old Trace Road into nine residential lots, which would allow for nine new single-family homes and seven smaller accessory units. The council also handled routine business like selecting new leadership and reviewing the director's report and meeting schedule.
keyTopics: Proposal to subdivide 1-acre lot into 9 residential lots plus accessory units on Old Trace Road
Nine new single-family homes and seven junior accessory dwelling units planned for the site
Election of new City Council Chair and Vice Chair
Project is exempt from environmental review under state law
